context: FreelanceFlow is a web application that helps freelancers create, send, and track invoices. It replaces manual spreadsheet-based invoicing with a streamlined digital workflow.
target_users: Freelance designers and developers aged 25–40 who work remotely, manage 3–10 active clients, and currently track invoices in Excel or Google Sheets.
primary_goal: Allow a freelancer to create and send a professional invoice to a client in under 5 minutes, and automatically track its payment status.
out_of_scope: Native mobile app (iOS / Android) — deferred to post-Beta
Stripe or payment gateway integration — deferred to V1.1
Multi-user / team accounts — deferred to V2
Accounting software integrations (QuickBooks, Xero) — deferred to V2
Tax calculation or VAT/GST handling — deferred to V1.1

stakeholders.name: Ian Vince
stakeholders.interest: Ship a working MVP that validates the invoicing pain point

stakeholders.name: Alpha Test Users
stakeholders.interest: Easy invoicing, fewer late payments, professional look

requirements.description: The business must reduce the average time a freelancer spends on invoicing from 3 hours/week to under 30 minutes/week
The system must allow a user to create an invoice with client name, line items, auto-calculated totals, and a due date
The system must allow a user to view a list of all invoices filtered by status (Draft / Sent / Paid / Overdue)
requirements.rationale: Users must be able to track what has happened to each invoice

requirements.description: The system must support email/password registration and login, with email verification
requirements.rationale: Users must have accounts before any personal data can be stored

requirements.description: Invoice list and dashboard must load within 2 seconds under normal load (< 500 concurrent users)
All passwords must be hashed. All data in transit must use HTTPS/TLS. OWASP Top 10 must be addressed before launch.
The tech stack is fixed: Next.js (App Router), Supabase (Auth + DB), Tailwind CSS, Vercel (hosting)
Users have basic digital literacy — they can fill in a web form, use a browser, and read an email

constraints: The tech stack is fixed: Next.js (App Router), Supabase (Auth + DB), Tailwind CSS, Vercel (hosting)
The MVP must be shipped within 6 weeks with a solo developer
The total operating budget for MVP is under $50/month
assumptions: Supabase free tier is sufficient for MVP load
Freelancers are willing to pay for a Pro plan if the free tier proves value
